label: Hospitals Damaged
note: Iran (IRCS/MoH via UNICEF): 442 health/medical facilities damaged; 763 schools damaged (OCHA/IRCS); 4 hospitals closed; 10 million affected by service disruption incl. 2.2 million children — WHO verified 23 attacks on Iranian healthcare Mar 1-Apr 2, 9 worker deaths — Lebanon: Tebnine Government Hospital damaged Apr 14 (≥5 killed); 147+ attacks on healthcare, 100+ health workers killed; 6 hospitals closed; 15 hospitals damaged; 51 PHCCs closed, 7 PHCCs damaged — 1,400+ buildings demolished in southern Lebanon since 2 March (BBC Verify satellite) — Bushehr ~30 consecutive days no incident — ICRC delivered first Iran shipment Apr 13-14 (171 MT via 14 trucks from Jordan, ~25,000 people served) — DAY 69 (May 7): WHO (Tedros, May 6) verifies 152 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon resulting in 103 deaths and 241 injuries since the conflict started; 3 hospitals + 41 primary health centers closed; 16 hospitals damaged. ISRAEL BREAKS LEBANON CEASEFIRE: IDF strike on Beirut's southern suburbs (Haret Hreik / Ghobeiri / Dahiyeh, May 6) killed Hezbollah Radwan Force Operations Commander Ahmed Ghaleb Ballout (also rendered Malek Ballout), Nasr regional intel chief Muhammad Ali Bazi, and Aerial Defense Chief Hussein Hassan — first Beirut strike since the April 17 ceasefire. 11+ killed in concurrent strikes across south/east Lebanon May 6–7. Lebanese MoPH cumulative since Mar 2: 2,727 killed / 8,438 wounded (CNN/MoPH May 7; +31 killed, +174 wounded since May 4); 177 children killed. NO new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory (ceasefire Day 29 holds; phase officially shifted to blockade + diplomacy per Rubio May 5). Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (FOIZ) fires from May 4 strike contained per UAE authorities; Habshan-Fujairah pipeline endpoint damage assessment ongoing (1.5 Mb/d ADCOP capacity at risk). Iran internet blackout entering Day 69 (~1,656+ hours per GlobalSecurity OPREP May 7); ~3M IDPs in Iran (HRANA estimate). Hapag-Lloyd statement May 7: Hormuz closure costing ~$60M/week in fuel + insurance. Goldman Sachs (Al Jazeera May 6): GCC states losing ~$700M/day on stranded oil exports. UAE NOTAM A1722/26 partial Emirates FIR closure remains in effect through May 11; Dubai/Sharjah schools on distance learning May 5–8 with reassessment scheduled May 8. — DAY 68 (May 6): No new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory (ceasefire Day 28 holds; Epic Fury 'concluded' per Rubio May 5). Project Freedom paused evening May 5 per Trump (Pakistani-mediated MOU progress); US naval blockade of Iranian ports REMAINS active. Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (VTTI) handles ~1.7M bbl/day — roughly half of UAE export capacity, the bypass route around Strait of Hormuz; response to May 4 fire continues. Habshan-Fujairah pipeline endpoint had been hit prior. UKMTO May 4 advisory: cargo vessel ~66km north of Dubai reported engine room fire (cause unknown). Iran oil-storage capacity assessed ~13 days of import-export disruption (US analyst); $435M/day estimated loss to Iranian economy. UAE airspace partial closure (NOTAM A1722/26) remains in effect through May 11; UAE schools remain on remote learning May 5-8 with reassessment scheduled May 8. Lebanese MoPH cumulative since Mar 2: 2,700+ killed / 8,311 wounded (May 5 figure per Palestine Chronicle citing MoPH; precise WAFA May 4: 2,696/8,264); UNHCR notes 380 Lebanese killed since Apr 17 ceasefire took effect. — DAY 67 (May 5): No new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory (ceasefire Day 27 holds; Pentagon Hegseth/Caine briefing confirms 'ceasefire is not over'). Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (VTTI) fire from May 4 Iranian drone strike continued response; UAE airspace partial closure (NOTAM A1722/26) in effect through May 11 — Emirates FIR restricted to narrow corridors, Al Dhafra Air Base remains operational on restricted routing; Dubai schools shifted to distance learning May 5-8. Lebanese MoPH cumulative since Mar 2: 2,659 killed / 8,183 wounded (May 3 figure; floor pending consolidation of May 3-4 strikes incl. >20 IDF Beqaa/south-Lebanon sites and 10+ village evacuation expansion north of Litani in Nabatieh district). Lebanon homes destroyed: ~50,000 (advocacy figure cited May 4). — DAY 66 (May 4): UAE INFRASTRUCTURE TARGETED FOR FIRST TIME SINCE 8 APR CEASEFIRE — Iran drone strike ignited fire at FUJAIRAH OIL INDUSTRY ZONE (VTTI facility, jointly owned by IFM/Vitol/TAQA) — endpoint of Habshan-Fujairah pipeline, 1.5 Mb/d capacity, UAE's only Hormuz-bypass crude export route; Civil Defense engaged; 3 Indian nationals moderately injured. ADNOC state-owned crude tanker 'Barakah' (empty) struck by 2x suicide drones in Hormuz transit area near UAE — UAE MFA formal accusation of Iranian 'terrorist attack' citing UNSCR 2817. UAE MoD intercepted 12 ballistic missiles + 3 cruise missiles + 4 UAVs (4th cruise missile fell to sea); first UAE missile-alert siren since 8 Apr ceasefire. UAE shifts schools to remote learning May 5-8. CENTCOM Project Freedom launched Day 66 — first US-flagged merchant vessels successfully transited Hormuz under armed escort (2 USN guided-missile destroyers in Persian Gulf, 100+ aircraft, 15,000 troops); US Navy sank 6 IRGC small boats, no US vessels hit. CENTCOM denied Iranian Fars News claim of US warship struck near Jask. LEBANON ESCALATION CODIFIED: IDF evacuation orders extended NORTH OF LITANI 03 May (10+ villages incl. Nabatieh District) — first major geographic expansion since 17 Apr Lebanon ceasefire; cumulative evacuation orders cover ~1,470 km² (≈14% of Lebanese territory). Lebanese government revised displacement figure UP to 1.6M (~25% of population) from prior ~1.0M baseline. Lebanon homes destroyed: 40,000+ (south Lebanon). Litani bridges destroyed: 9; Qasmieh Bridge sole operable main crossing (damaged but operable). 04 May IDF struck >20 Hezbollah-linked sites Beqaa Valley + south Lebanon: Deir Siryan-Zawtar, Kounine (Bint Jbeil), Baraachit, Kafra, Jannata, Deir Qanoun al-Nahr, Kharayeb (Zahrani). Netanyahu 04 May: 'striking now in security zone, north of security zone, and north of the Litani… [Hezbollah] has about 10% of the missiles it had at beginning of war.' Iran's Basij Construction Org (state media 03 May) claims 12,000+ Iranian residential buildings repaired (UNVERIFIED, likely overstated for domestic morale). US base damage cumulative: 16 American military sites damaged (CNN 1 May); equipment destroyed $2.3-2.8B (Al Jazeera); infrastructure repair >$5B (AEI); Fifth Fleet HQ ~$200M; 1 of 9 global AN/TPY-2 THAAD radars destroyed at Muwaffaq Salti (Jordan), $300M. Targets struck Iran cumulative: 13,000+ (US official, end of 38-day combat ops). — DAY 64 (May 2): NO VERIFIED NEW INFRASTRUCTURE DAMAGE on Iranian territory; Iran ceasefire holds Day 24. LEBANON CONTINUES WORSENING: Israeli drone strikes on Kfar Dajjal and Shoukin (May 2) killed 4 civilians; airstrikes hit Froun, Adshit, Majdal Zoun, Ghandouriyeh, Zawtar al-Sharqiyah, Harouf, Mleeta, Habboush, and Wadi al-Salouqi (Times of Israel liveblog May 2). Habboush death toll from May 1 strikes rose to 8. IDF issued evacuation orders for 9 villages. Lebanese MoPH cumulative since Mar 2 reconciled to 2,618 killed / 8,094 wounded after May 1 strikes (10-12 killed Habboush 6 + 3 villages 4) and ongoing May 2 strikes (4-8 killed). Hezbollah fired rocket at IDF troops in Lebanon (intercepted) and explosive drone fell in northern Israel — no injuries. New 24h damage: mosque + religious building Zawtar al-Sharqiyah; multiple homes Habboush; civilian areas Kfar Dajjal/Shoukin. — DAY 63 (May 1): NO VERIFIED NEW INFRASTRUCTURE DAMAGE. Iran ceasefire holds Day 23; no kinetic strikes on Iranian territory. Lebanese MoPH cumulative since Mar 2 reconciled to 2,534 killed / 7,863 wounded (Al Jazeera Apr 29) after Apr 28 Israeli strike on Majdal Zoun killed 3 Lebanese Civil Defence rescuers (Hussein Sati, Hadi Daher, Hussein Ghadbouni) and 2 civilians. Cumulative damage figures (largely White-House-sourced, disputed by ISW/independent BDA): 85% of Iranian defense industrial base destroyed; 70% of Iranian space-launch facilities/ground stations; entire Iranian submarine fleet sunk; 97% of Iranian naval mines destroyed; 150 warships across 16 classes destroyed. Trump (Apr 29 Axios): Lebanon strikes must be 'surgical'; ceasefire fragile, mid-May expiry remains the operational risk. — DAY 62 (Apr 30): LEBANON STRIKES WIDEN — AT LEAST 9-14 KILLED IN SOUTHERN LEBANON. APR 30: Lebanese National News Agency reports at least 14 killed in Israeli air strikes across southern Lebanon (Al Jazeera live blog cites 14; CNN/Reuters initial reports cited at least 9). IDF cited Hezbollah infrastructure under ceasefire's 'imminent threat' carve-out — 3 days after Apr 27 IDF Bekaa Valley expansion (>20 sites). Lebanese MoPH cumulative now pushes toward 2,520 (baseline 2,509 Apr 27 + minimum +9). NO new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory Apr 30 (Iran ceasefire holding — Day 22 of indefinite extension). Iran energy-conservation regime: Apr 29 government instructed offices to cut electricity use up to 70% after 1 p.m.; household discounts for usage cuts launched. Oil Minister Mohsen Paknejad called conservation 'religious duty' but dismissed blockade impact. Iran economy (Quincy Institute via CNN Apr 28): 50% of Iranian jobs at risk; additional 5% of population pushed into poverty; per-capita income $8,000 (2012) → $5,000 (2024) pre-war. — DAY 61 (Apr 29): LEBANON CEASEFIRE COLLAPSING IN SLOW MOTION — IDF CHIEF ZAMIR DECLARES 'NO CEASEFIRE' IN SOUTH LEBANON. APR 28: Israeli strikes kill at least 8 across southern Lebanon despite declared ceasefire — three Lebanese Civil Defense volunteers (Hussein Sati, Hadi Daher, Hussein Ghadbouni) killed during rescue operation, 2 LAF soldiers wounded; IDF says it destroyed 'Gaza-like' Hezbollah underground tunnels in southern Lebanon; IDF orders evacuation of 16 ADDITIONAL Lebanese towns/villages. APR 29: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir on Lebanon border tour — 'no ceasefire' in south Lebanon as fight against Hezbollah continues; Hezbollah drone shot down by IAF over Misgav Am border community, second suspicious aerial target intercepted over southern Lebanon; sirens sounded twice in Misgav Am. CTP-ISW Apr 28 cites Israeli media reporting Hezbollah drones have caused 'dozens of IDF casualties in recent weeks.' APR 26: Iran banned export of all steel products effective Apr 26 (Fars/AFP via CBS) — civilian-economic ripple of war indicating damage to Iranian industrial base. APR 28: Iran reactivated 30-year-old VLCC for floating oil storage (CTP-ISW); Kpler estimates 12-22 days of storage capacity remaining before tanks fill. NO new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory Apr 28-29 (Iran ceasefire holding, Day 21). DAY 60 (Apr 28): LEBANON CEASEFIRE FRAYING — IDF EXPANDS INTO BEKAA VALLEY. Israeli military said it hit >20 Hezbollah-linked weapons-manufacturing, storage, and rocket-launch sites across the Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon Apr 27 — strikes near Nabi Chit (eastern border with Syria) marked first such expansion since the Trump-brokered Lebanon ceasefire took effect Apr 16; Lebanese state media reported ≥3 wounded across the south. Hezbollah claimed drone attack on an IDF tank; IDF said the drone exploded near troops without casualties (tank-impact UNVERIFIED). Lebanese Health Ministry cumulative Apr 28: 2,509 killed / 7,755+ wounded (+18 vs Apr 21 baseline). No new strike-driven infrastructure damage on Iranian territory Apr 27-28 (ceasefire holding, Day 18); Bekaa Valley industrial sites damaged in IDF Apr 27 strikes — sectoral counts not yet released by Lebanese authorities. — DAY 59 (Apr 27): LEBANON CEASEFIRE — DEADLIEST DAY SINCE TRUCE INCEPTION. Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon (Apr 26) killed 14 (incl. 2 women, 2 children) and wounded 37 across Yohmor al-Shaqeef, Safad al-Battikh, Kfar Tibnit, Zawtar al-Sharqiyah, Nabatieh al-Fawqa, Kafra and others — Lebanese MoPH cumulative: 2,521 killed / 7,750 wounded since Mar 2 (incl. 277 women, 177 children, 100 medics killed); ≥36 killed since Apr 16/17 ceasefire began (AFP). Religious site damage: a mosque and another religious building in Zawtar al-Sharqiyah destroyed (Lebanese NNA). One IDF soldier killed in southern Lebanon combat (cumulative IDF KIA in Lebanon now 16); 6 IDF wounded (4 severely). IDF intercepted 3 drones inbound from Lebanon shortly after Netanyahu cabinet remarks ('Hezbollah's violations are, in practice, dismantling the ceasefire'). Hezbollah characterized actions as 'a legitimate response to the enemy's persistent violations.' IDF ordered evacuations Apr 26-27 of 7 villages north of the Litani River and beyond the so-called 'yellow line' — AFP correspondents reported 'heavy traffic heading north'; affected towns near Kunin, Bint Jbeil, Deir Aames previously cited. APR 27: IDF raid on Kafra entrance, road interdiction at dawn (Lebanese NNA). No new infrastructure damage in Iran proper Apr 27 (Iran ceasefire holding, Day 19). Reuters notes Russia is building two new nuclear units at Bushehr (carry-forward, not Apr 27 event). DAY 58 (Apr 26): LEBANON CEASEFIRE FRAYING — Sgt. Idan Fooks (19, 7th Armored Brigade/77th Battalion, Petah Tikva) killed by Hezbollah explosive drone in southern Lebanon — third IDF KIA since Apr 17 truce; 1 officer + 3 troops seriously wounded, 1 moderately, 1 lightly. Netanyahu publicly declared Hezbollah was 'disintegrating' the ceasefire ('we have eliminated 46 terrorists [past two weeks]... we will act with a strong hand and an outstretched arm'). IDF overnight airstrikes on Hezbollah rocket launchers at Deir El Zahrani, Reman, Al-Saamiya, Nabatiye al-Faouqa killed 6 (AFP photo confirmed); Lebanese MoPH cumulative running toll above 2,500 killed (consolidated 2,502) / 7,725 wounded. Lebanon PM Salam reportedly seeking emergency US intercession. Post-ceasefire (since Apr 17) cumulative: ~201 killed, ~181 wounded in Lebanon. Trump cancelled Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan envoy mission Apr 25; reverted to phone-only diplomacy. Tehran IKIA international flights continued normal operations Day 58. DAY 57 (Apr 25): TEHRAN IKIA INTERNATIONAL FLIGHTS RESUMED after 56-day shutdown (Istanbul, Muscat, Medina departures confirmed; Iran Air domestic Tehran-Mashhad inaugural); Israeli strikes killed 4 in Yohmor al-Shaqeef (Nabatieh district, Lebanon) per Lebanese MoPH — Lebanese cumulative running toll 2,495 killed / 7,719 wounded; IDF intercepted a 'suspicious aerial target' in southern Lebanon; Pezeshkian publicly urged Iranians to halve home electricity use ('two lights instead of ten') signaling continued grid damage; Russian ambassador (Apr 24) said Russia hopes work on Bushehr Units 2 & 3 completion will resume — Rosatom staff reportedly returning; Bushehr NPP at 21 consecutive days no new incident (last strike Apr 4); CNN satellite imagery comparison Apr 14 → Apr 23 shows Bint Jbeil (Lebanon) town center 'completely flattened.' DAY 56 (Apr 24): UN HUMAN RIGHTS OFFICE (OHCHR Geneva) said it has documented 'patterns of attacks on civilians in populated areas and residential buildings in Lebanon and Israel that may amount to serious violations of international humanitarian law' — strongest UN legal posture of the war to date; PENTAGON TOLD CONGRESS (briefing Apr 22) that clearing Hormuz of Iranian mines could take ~6 MONTHS even after hostilities end (DIA assessment range: 1-6 months); Israeli air strike on Touline (southern Lebanon) killed 2 civilians; IDF killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in Bint Jbeil exchange of fire (also struck rocket launchers in Yater and Kafra); Hezbollah shot down 1 Israeli drone, attacked IDF APC in Ramyah 'in response to home demolitions'; Lebanese MoPH cumulative toll Apr 24: 2,491 killed / 7,719 wounded (since Mar 2); Iranian authorities announced Tehran's Imam Khomeini Airport will resume international flights Apr 25 starting with Istanbul and Muscat routes; Trump extended Jones Act waiver 90 days. DAY 55: NO NEW KINETIC DAMAGE on Iran front Apr 22-23 (ceasefire extended INDEFINITELY by Trump ~16:22 ET Apr 21); Apr 23 LEBANESE JOURNALIST KILLED — Al-Akhbar correspondent Amal Khalil killed in Israeli strike on village of al-Tiri, southern Lebanon (PM calls 'war crime'; UN and CPJ condemn); 4-5 total killed in southern Lebanon Apr 22-23; IDF Commando Brigade killed 2 armed Hezbollah operatives near Aynata/Bint Jbeil Apr 23; Iranian Red Crescent Apr 23 claims US-seized cargo ship was carrying 'lifesaving medical supplies'; multiple oil spills visible from space after strikes on oil facilities and ships (CNN Apr 23); International Transport Workers' Federation received 1,900 requests for assistance from seafarers stranded in Persian Gulf since war began; IMO (UN maritime agency) condemned ship attacks/seizures Apr 23; Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin acquitted after 52-day detention. DAY 54: Debel crucifix investigation CONCLUDED Apr 21 — IDF jailed soldier who struck statue 30 days + removed from combat duty, same penalty for soldier who photographed him, 6 others face further review; IDF REPLACED statue Apr 21 in full coordination with Debel community (new metallic Jesus + 4 saint paintings); Cardinal Pizzaballa: 'unreserved condemnation'; Netanyahu (Apr 20): 'stunned and saddened'; Iranian President Pezeshkian also condemned the desecration
source: Iranian Red Crescent / Iranian Health Ministry / WHO / OCHA / Lebanese MoPH / ICRC / WAFA / Al Jazeera
detail: Iran infrastructure: 115,000+ civilian buildings damaged; 309 health facilities + 42 ambulances damaged (IRCS); 7 Iranian hospitals evacuated; 700+ educational facilities damaged, 30+ universities struck; 120+ historical sites reported damaged; partial power blackouts confirmed in Tehran and Karaj by Iran's energy minister; Pasteur Institute (Iran's premier public health facility, inoperable), Khatam-al-Anbia and Gandhi hospitals damaged; Delaram Sina Psychiatric Hospital, Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical, Shahid Mutahhari Hospital destroyed. Bushehr NPP: no new strikes Apr 16-17; Apr 4 projectile strike (4th incident) killed one physical-protection staffer and damaged an auxiliary building — no radiation increase. Iran submitted incidents via INFCIRC/1353 Apr 9. IAEA DG Grossi remains 'deeply concerned' and continues invoking the 'Seven Indispensable Pillars.' AEOI head Eslami refuses inspections of struck facilities pending codified protocols for 'internationally protected facilities subjected to military attacks.' Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone struck Apr 4 (5 killed, 170 injured). All Iranian ports blockaded since Apr 13; Iran's National Petrochemical Co suspended all petrochemical exports 'until further notice' Apr 15. ICRC delivered its first shipment into Iran Apr 14 — 171 metric tonnes via 14 trucks from Jordan, serving ~25,000 people (first since war start). Lebanon (Lebanese MoPH via WAFA/Al Jazeera Apr 17): 2,167-2,196 killed / 7,061 wounded; 172 children, 250+ women, 93 health workers killed. WHO Lebanon: 133 attacks on healthcare; 80+ health-worker deaths; 55 primary care centers and 6 hospitals closed; 14 hospitals damaged. OCHA Lebanon flash appeal seeks $308.3M through May. UNHCR reports 202,477+ cross-border flows from Lebanon into Syria since Mar 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals). UNICEF Rapid Response Mechanism in Lebanon has reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. Qaaqaiyet el Jisr and Delaffi bridges plus Litani crossings destroyed — isolating the south. 472 schools repurposed as shelters. Sultaniye power station damaged; water pipelines in Marjaayoun hit. Battle of Bint Jbeil: IDF 98th Division consolidated the sector — Apr 16 Egoz Unit raid on Hezbollah 'combat compound' in Aynata casbah seized RPGs, Kalashnikovs, grenades, an anti-aircraft missile, and surveillance gear; IDF demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation. Separately IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags. Cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed. IDF strike on Nabatieh Apr 16. Lebanon ceasefire effective 5:00 PM ET Apr 16; tens of thousands of IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite IDF spokesman Adraee's warning not to return south of the Litani yet. WHO Flash Appeal $30M for 5 most-affected countries (incl. $10M for Lebanon). WHO Lebanon mobilized 60+ tonnes of medical supplies since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge. Displacement: Lebanon >1.2M internal (~20% of population); Iran up to 3.2M IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12, likely stale); 4.4M Afghan refugees at elevated risk. Source: WHO / IRCS / IAEA / OCHA / UNHCR / HRANA / UNICEF / Hengaw / NBC News / Lebanese MoPH / WAFA / Al Jazeera / ICRC.

label: Schools Destroyed
note: 763 schools damaged per IRCS/OCHA (Day 54); 481 Iranian schools damaged (USCRI earlier baseline); 700+ educational facilities affected; 65+ schools destroyed per OCHA; 115,193 civilian housing units damaged in Iran (IRCS). 30+ universities struck including Shahid Beheshti University (Apr 3), Sharif University AI data center. Minab school (165-180 killed). 115,000+ civilian buildings damaged. 130+ historical/cultural heritage sites damaged incl. Golestan Palace and Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square (UNESCO). Rafi-Nia Synagogue in central Tehran destroyed in adjacent strike during Passover. Lebanon: IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags Apr 16. IRGC threatened retaliatory strikes on US/Israeli campuses in Qatar and UAE.
source: Iranian Red Crescent / AP / NYT / Amnesty
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval compound. Amnesty International verified 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) at Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school on February 28. US internal investigation (Mar 11) determined the strike was likely due to outdated DIA intelligence — the school was once part of an IRGC compound but had been walled off since 2016. Weapons experts confirmed Tomahawk missile fragments. UN investigation initiated March 17, ongoing. HRW and Amnesty demanded accountability for potential war crimes.

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: IEA: 44+ energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries + Kharg Island 50+ targets Apr 7 + Asaluyeh (South Pars, ~50% of petrochemical production) — worst since combined 1973 + 1979 oil shocks
source: IEA / DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: IEA (Mar 23): At least 44 energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — 'very severe, worse than the combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks.' IEA released record 400M barrels from strategic stockpiles (US: 172M from SPR). Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields (production collapsed from 4.2M to ~1.2-1.4M bpd). Combined Gulf production cuts exceed 10M bpd (JPMorgan). Iran: Kharg destroyed, South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 struck. Qatar: Ras Laffan 12.8 MTPA lost (17% of LNG exports, 3-5yr repair). SAMREF (Saudi) struck. Kuwait: Mina al-Ahmadi hit. Haifa BAZAN refinery. Bahrain Bapco under force majeure. Iran sanctions lifted on ~140M barrels at sea (30 days). Jones Act waived 60 days.

label: Nuclear Facilities Struck
note: Fordow, Isfahan UCF, Natanz (3x), Bushehr (4x), Arak Heavy Water, Ardakan yellowcake (struck again Apr 7 — Shahid Rezayee Nejad facility), Yazd, Khondab (severely damaged, no longer operational) — IAEA zero access — Grossi: 'cannot rule out radiological release necessitating evacuation of areas as large as major cities'
detail: Fordow enrichment facility struck Day 1 with B-2/GBU-57 MOPs — assessed inoperable. Isfahan UCF struck Day 1. Natanz struck 4 times — underground FEP 'severely damaged.' Day 29: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Ardakan yellowcake and Khondab Heavy Water Complex. Bushehr struck 4 times — most recently Apr 4 when 1 physical protection staff killed, nearest strike impacted 75m from site perimeter. IAEA DG Grossi: strikes could cause 'severe radiological accident with consequences beyond Iran's borders.' Rosatom evacuated 198 Russian staff from Bushehr Apr 4. Iran possesses 440.9 kg of 60%-enriched uranium — enough for approximately 10 nuclear weapons if further enriched. IAEA has zero access to enrichment facilities. Grossi: 'I do not believe that Iran's nuclear program can be eliminated solely by military action.' Source: IAEA / Rosatom / Pentagon / Bulletin of Atomic Scientists.

label: Airports Damaged
note: Iran + region — 3 Tehran airports struck Apr 7 (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh); DAY 57 (Apr 25): Tehran IKIA RESUMES international flights after 56-day hiatus — first departures to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina; Iran Air domestic Tehran-Mashhad inaugural; Mehrabad continues domestic + select diplomatic ops
source: ACLED / Flightradar24 / Iranian state media / Al Jazeera / FlightRadar24
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. April 7: strikes on three Tehran airports (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh) targeting Iranian air force aircraft and helicopters. Regional airspace closures affect 52,000+ commercial flights. DAY 57 (Apr 25): Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport (IKIA) resumed international flights after a 56-day shutdown — Iran state TV confirmed departures to Istanbul, Muscat (Oman), and Medina (Saudi Arabia); FlightRadar24 showed at least four Istanbul-bound departures Saturday morning. Iran Air operated its inaugural post-war domestic flight Tehran-Mashhad. Routes to Baku, Najaf, Baghdad, and Doha scheduled in coming days. Iran Airports & Air Navigation CEO Mohammad Amirani indicated eastern Iran (Mashhad, Zahedan, Kerman, Yazd, Birjand) will be prioritised for transit. Mehrabad continues domestic + limited diplomatic ops.

label: Bridges Destroyed
note: Israel launched wide-scale bridge/railway strikes Apr 7 — Qom bridge, Kashan railway bridge (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan freeway, Karaj railway — IDF issued 12-hour Farsi warning to avoid trains — Karaj B1 bridge double-tap Apr 2 (8 killed, 95 wounded) — Qasmieh Bridge (last south of Litani) destroyed Apr 8, partially reopened, struck again Apr 16 cutting humanitarian access — all Litani River bridges destroyed in Lebanon (150,000 cut off)
source: Satellite imagery / IDF / Lebanese media / Iranian state media
detail: April 7: IDF launched 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' — confirmed hits on key bridge near Qom, railway bridge in Kashan (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan freeway, railway infrastructure in Karaj. IDF issued 'urgent warning' in Farsi to stay away from trains/railways for 12 hours. Appears to be opening phase of Trump's 'Bridge Day.' Previous bridges destroyed in Kermanshah, Lorestan, Khuzestan provinces. Karaj B1 bridge Apr 2 double-tap killed 8, wounded 95. Lebanon: All Litani River bridges destroyed — 150,000 people cut off in southern Lebanon. Qasmieh Bridge (last bridge south of Litani) destroyed Apr 8, partially reopened, struck again Apr 16 cutting humanitarian access. IDF permanent buffer zone south of Litani.
